channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: The Jacksonville Jaguars say their flight to London will be delayed because of Hurricane Milton
item.description: The Jacksonville Jaguars plan to arrive to London later than expected Friday because of Hurricane Milton, the team confirmed ahead of Sunday’s game against the Chicago Bears.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sei Young Kim cards 10-under 62 to lead LPGA event in China after first round
item.description: Sei Young Kim, looking for her first victory this season, opened with a 10-under 62 to lead the first round of the Buick LPGA Shanghai tournament.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Giancarlo Stanton hits go-ahead homer in the eighth, Yankees beat Royals 3-2 in Game 3 of the ALDS
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Giancarlo Stanton hit a go-ahead homer in the eighth inning amid a battle of bullpens, and the New York Yankees beat the Kansas City Royals 3-2 on Wednesday night in Game 3 of their AL Division Series at Kauffman Stadium.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lions' Dan Campbell hilariously recalls moment when player punched him as he walked through team headquarters
item.description: Detroit Lions star center Frank Ragnow was injured during a game in September, but he appeared to be eager to prove to his head coach he was ready to return to action.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fruita's Olivia Wirth gets family support on senior night
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Growing up the youngest of three in a competitive, athletic household, Fruita Monument senior Olivia Wirth learned the value of hard work from her parents and siblings.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Tigers blank Guardians 3-0 in ALDS with clutch hitting and strong pitching, taking 2-1 series lead
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Riley Greene and Spencer Torkelson each drove in a run, and six pitchers combined to lift the Detroit Tigers to a 3-0 win over Cleveland Guardians on Wednesday and a 2-1 lead in their AL Division Series.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL investigating George Pickens' explicit message on eye black during Steelers game: report
item.description: The NFL is reportedly investigating Pittsburgh Steelers wide receiver George Pickens for having an explicit message on his eye black against the Dallas Cowboys.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LeBron James meets 'lifesaver' who performed CPR on son Bronny
item.description: In an emotional moment, LeBron James recently met the "lifesaver" who performed CPR on his son, Bronny, after he went into cardiac arrest.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yankees' Juan Soto focused solely on the playoffs, even though a lucrative offseason looms
item.description: Yankees slugger Juan Soto says he is focused solely on helping New York win the World Series, and not thinking at all about a potentially lucrative offseason.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Making way for the A’s: Las Vegas blows a kiss goodbye — literally — to the Tropicana with a flashy casino implosion
item.description: the implosion cleared land for a $1.5 billion baseball stadium for the relocating Oakland Athletics, part of the city’s latest rebrand into a sports hub.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Mets postseason tickets outsell Yankees on StubHub
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The Mets and Yankees are both looking to make a run this postseason, but the Amazins are selling more tickets than the Bronx Bombers, according to StubHub. The Mets-Phillies National League Division Series was the best-selling series of the 2024 postseason on StubHub.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Jürgen Klopp to take on new role as head of global soccer at Red Bull
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Former Liverpool coach Jürgen Klopp is returning to soccer after a shorter than expected break. Energy drinks manufacturer Red Bull announced Wednesday that the German mentor is to become its head of global soccer from January, overseeing its international network of clubs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lindor relishes his 1st home game in a month as Mets trounce Phillies for 2-1 lead in NLDS
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Francisco Lindor waited a month to get back in the lineup at Citi Field. When he finally did Tuesday, was it everything he envisioned? “It was better,” Lindor said, flashing that signature smile. “Pretty cool. Pretty special." Playing at home for the first time since Sept.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah Hockey Club celebrates opening game with evening-long Delta Center plaza party
item.description: Just 172 days after it was announced Utah would be joining the National Hockey League, the Utah Hockey Club is set to play its first game in its inaugural season.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Grimace takes the 7 train to the Mets game
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Like many other Mets fans, Grimace took the No. 7 train to the Mets game at Citi Field Tuesday. The Amazins sold out Citi Field for Game 3 of the National League Division Series against the Phillies. Given the crowd, fans were encouraged to take public transit to the game.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: What is Freddie Freeman's status for NLDS Game 3?
item.description: Perhaps the most understated but significant development in Sunday night’s emotionally charged National League Division Series game between San Diego and Los Angeles was the departure of Dodgers first baseman Freddie Freeman.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns scores 10 points in abbreviated preseason debut with New York Knicks
item.description: Jalen Brunson thought twice about going 1-on-2 in transition, instead dribbled back outside and flipped the ball behind him to Karl-Anthony Towns, who made a 3-pointer with a defender in his face.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Baseball fans discuss Dodgers’ chances in NLDS Game 2 over donuts
item.description: After a rollercoaster of emotions in Game 1 of the National League Divisional Series, baseball lovers were up early getting donuts in Inglewood and eager to share their thoughts with the news on Sunday's matchup between the Los Angeles Dodgers and the San Diego Padres.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Bears vs. Panthers: What to know for Sunday's Week 5 matchup at Soldier Field
item.description: Carolina (1-3) at Chicago (2-2) Sunday, Noon, Fox UPDATE: The Bears announced their list of inactives, via their X feed. They are WR Velus Jones Jr., FB Khari Blasingame, DL Zacch Pickens, DB Terrell Smith and DL Dominique Robinson.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ohtani homers in playoff debut and the Dodgers rally to beat the Padres 7-5 in NLDS opener
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani hit a three-run homer in his postseason debut, Teoscar Hernández’s two-run single gave Los Angeles its first lead in a playoff game in two years, and the Dodgers beat the San Diego Padres 7-5 in their NL Division Series opener Saturday.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Verdugo's go-ahead single and acrobatic catch lift Yankees over Royals 6-5 in ALDS opener
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Alex Verdugo hit a tiebreaking single in the seventh inning and saved at least one run with a sliding catch along the left-field line, boosting the New York Yankees over the Kansas City Royals 6-5 on Saturday night in their AL Division Series opener.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers' Kershaw, Guardians' Lively, Tigers' Mize and Yankees' Stroman are left off ALDS rosters
item.description: Pitchers Clayton Kershaw and Joe Kelly were left off the Los Angeles Dodgers' roster for their NL Division Series against the San Diego Padres that started Saturday. San Diego added right-handers Martín Pérez and Alek Jacob, who were skipped from the Wild Card Series roster against Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz counting on Hendricks and George to take big strides
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - With its commitment to a youth movement, the Utah Jazz are counting on Keyonte George and Taylor Hendricks to both take big strides in their second seasons this year. After playing in 75 games as a rookie, George wore down a bit at the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: How to watch Padres, Dodgers in NLDS
item.description: The Padres' postseason journey continues as the team gets ready to play their bitter rivals, the Los Angeles Dodgers, in the National League Division Series.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5
